topic.name: Historical Development of American Cities
topic.description: This topic covers the historical growth and cultural significance of various American cities and their architectural landmarks, highlighting the contributions of diverse communities.

Document 1: African Americans in Omaha are central to the development and growth of the 43rd largest city in the United States.
Omaha attracted the most African-American migrants from the South in the Great Migration of the early 20th century.
Document 2: Kirkpatrick Chapel is among the university's oldest extant buildings.
The chapel was built in 1873 when Rutgers was a small, private liberal arts college.
Document 3: The Grade I listed buildings in Somerset demonstrate the history and diversity of its architecture.
Grade I structures are considered to be buildings of exceptional interest.
Document 4: The history of human activity in Indiana began with migratory tribes of Native Americans.
Indiana was admitted to the Union in 1816 as the nineteenth state.
Document 5: Leicester Abbey was founded in the 12th century and grew to become the wealthiest religious establishment within Leicestershire.
The abbey provided a home to an average of 30 to 40 canons.
Document 6: The architecture of Leeds encompasses a wide range of architectural styles and notable buildings.
Much of Leeds' prominent architecture is of the Victorian era.
Document 7: Dayton is the sixth-largest city in Ohio and is the county seat of Montgomery County.
Dayton plays host to significant research and development in fields like industrial, aeronautical, and astronautical engineering.

topic.name: Species Descriptions
topic.description: The documents provide detailed descriptions and classifications of various species across different biological categories.

1: Collybia cirrhata is a species of fungus in the Tricholomataceae family
The species was first described in the scientific literature in 1786
2: The smalltail shark (Carcharhinus porosus) is a species of requiem shark
It inhabits shallow waters close to shore
3: The slender smooth-hound or gollumshark (Gollum attenuatus) is a species of ground shark
It is endemic to the waters around New Zealand
4: Postelsia palmaeformis, also known as the sea palm, is a species of kelp
It is found along the western coast of North America
5: Drosera anglica, commonly known as the English sundew, is a carnivorous plant species
It is a temperate species with a generally circumboreal range
6: The Danube crested newt or Danube newt (Triturus dobrogicus) is a species of newt
It has a smaller and more slender body than the other crested newts in genus Triturus
7: The Bedlington Terrier is a breed of small dog named after the mining town of Bedlington
Originally bred to hunt vermin in mines

topic.name: Contemporary Pop Music
topic.description: The documents discuss various contemporary pop songs and their production, themes, and reception.

Document 7: Primary Colours is the second album by Australian garage punk band Eddy Current Suppression Ring.
The album was recorded in a suburb of Melbourne over a 24-hour period in August 2007.

topic.name: Military Aircraft and Operations
topic.description: The documents discuss various military aircraft, their designs, roles, and historical significance in different conflicts.

Document 1: Heinkel He 111 was a German aircraft designed as a fast medium bomber.
The He 111 was used as a strategic bomber during the Battle of Britain.
Document 2: The Avro Vulcan was operated by the Royal Air Force from 1956 until 1984.
The Vulcan was the backbone of the United Kingdom’s airborne nuclear deterrent during the Cold War.
Document 4: The Republic F-105 Thunderchief was a supersonic fighter-bomber used by the United States Air Force.
The F-105 conducted the majority of strike bombing missions during the early years of the Vietnam War.
Document 5: The General Aircraft Hamilcar was designed to carry heavy cargo in support of airborne troops during World War II.

topic.name: Professional Sports Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss various professional athletes in baseball and cricket, highlighting their careers and achievements.

Document 1: Michael Wacha is an American professional baseball pitcher for the St. Louis Cardinals of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Wacha earned the 2013 National League Championship Series Most Valuable Player Award.
Document 2: Two first-class cricket matches billed as 'Smokers v Non-Smokers' were played during the 1880s.
The first match was won by the Non-Smokers, while in the second, which was drawn, the Non-Smokers scored a then-record 803 runs.
Document 3: Jerry West is an American retired basketball player who played his entire professional career for the Los Angeles Lakers of the National Basketball Association (NBA).
West was a standout at East Bank High School and at West Virginia University.
Document 4: Wilton H. St Hill was a West Indian cricketer who played in West Indies' first Test match during their inaugural Test tour of England.
St Hill was highly regarded in Trinidad.
Document 5: Eric Hacker is an American professional baseball pitcher who plays for the NC Dinos of the Korea Baseball Organization.
Hacker was drafted out of Duncanville High School by the New York Yankees in 2002.
Document 6: Matt Kemp is an American professional baseball outfielder for the San Diego Padres of Major League Baseball (MLB).
He has been named to two All-Star teams and has won two Gold Glove Awards.
Document 7: Mervyn Roye Harvey was a cricketer who played in one Test match for Australia in 1947.
Harvey was described by Neil as 'the greatest cricketer of us all'.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various forms of transportation infrastructure, including highways, railways, and expressways in different regions.

Document 1: Illinois Route 22 is an east – west state highway in northeastern Illinois.
It has been largely modernized to deal with heavy traffic.
Document 2: Yatton railway station is on the Bristol to Exeter Line.
It provides all train services at the station.
Document 3: The Hanlon Expressway is a high-capacity at-grade suburban limited-access road.
It connects Highway 401 with the city of Guelph.
Document 4: The Magnificent Mile serves as the main thoroughfare between Chicago's Loop business district and its Gold Coast.
It is Chicago's largest shopping district.
Document 5: The E. C. Row Expressway is a municipal expressway in the city of Windsor.
It divides the city in half as it crosses it between the Ojibway Parkway and Banwell Road.
Document 6: West Creek is a tributary of Fishing Creek, in Pennsylvania.
It has the highest level of biodiversity of any stream in the upper Fishing Creek watershed.
Document 7: The Hell – Sunnan Line is a railway line between Hell, Stjørdal and Sunnan, Steinkjer in Norway.
It is the most heavily trafficked non-electrified line in Norway.

topic.name: Political and Social Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses the evolution and protection of individual rights, political freedoms, and social justice throughout history, particularly in the context of legal and political figures and movements.

Document 1: The First Amendment to the United States Constitution prohibits the making of any law respecting an establishment of religion
Beginning with Gitlow v. New York (1925), the Supreme Court applied the First Amendment to states
Document 3: Wilders is best known for his criticism of Islam; his views have made him a controversial figure
Wilders actively participated in the negotiations, resulting in a 'support agreement'
Document 6: Eleanor Roosevelt was an American politician, diplomat, and activist
President Harry S. Truman later called her the 'First Lady of the World' in tribute to her human rights achievements
Document 5: New Labour refers to a period in the history of the British Labour Party
The 'New Labour' brand was developed to regain trust from the electorate

topic.name: Naval Warfare History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical development, design, and operational roles of various naval vessels from different nations during significant conflicts, particularly focusing on World War I and World War II.

Document 1: The German aircraft carrier Graf Zeppelin was the lead ship in a class of two carriers of the same name ordered by the Kriegsmarine.
Graf Zeppelin was not completed and was never operational due to shifting construction priorities necessitated by the war.
Document 2: HMS Resolution was a Royal Sovereign-class pre-dreadnought battleship of the Royal Navy.
She was decommissioned in August 1911 and laid up at Motherbank for disposal.
Document 3: HMS Black Prince was the third ship of that name to serve with the Royal Navy.
Black Prince spent her active career with the Channel Fleet and was hulked in 1896.
Document 4: HMS Hereward was an H-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy in the mid-1930s.
Later that month, she was bombed and sunk by German dive bombers as she was evacuating Allied troops from Crete.
Document 5: France was the last ship of the Courbet-class battleships, the first dreadnoughts built for the French Navy.
She spent the war in the Mediterranean, covering the Otranto Barrage in the Adriatic.
Document 6: Vérité was a pre-dreadnought battleship of the Liberté class built by the French Navy.
After the end of the war, she was stricken from the naval register and broken up for scrap in 1922.
Document 7: Chervona Ukraina was an Admiral Nakhimov-class light cruiser of the Soviet Navy assigned to the Black Sea Fleet.
She was raised in 1947 and was used as a training hulk before becoming a target ship in 1950.

topic.name: Rowing Competitions and Gender Inclusion
topic.description: The documents discuss the history and significance of rowing competitions, particularly the Boat Race between Oxford and Cambridge, along with the inclusion of women in competitive sports.

Document 1: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Oxford went into the race as reigning champions, having beaten Cambridge by a canvas in the previous year's race.
Document 2: The Women's Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between Oxford University Women's Boat Club and Cambridge University Women's Boat Club that has taken place since 1927.
Oxford won the race by one and three-quarter lengths in a time of 7 minutes 11 seconds, their first win since the 2011 race.
Document 3: Wojdan Shaherkani was one of two women selected to represent Saudi Arabia at the 2012 Summer Olympics.
Her inclusion in the games came after the International Olympic Committee put pressure on the Saudi Arabian Olympic Committee to include women on their team.

topic.name: State Highways in the US
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways across different US states, detailing their routes, history, and geographical significance.

Document 1: M-45 is a state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan that is also called Lake Michigan Drive.
The road runs through rural and suburban areas of Ottawa and Kent counties.
Document 2: Maryland Route 135 is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
MD 135 is the main link between the towns of Oakland, Mountain Lake Park, Loch Lynn Heights, and Deer Park.
Document 3: State Route 372 is a very short, two-lane east – west state highway in the southern part of the U.S. state of Ohio.
The primary function of SR 372 has been to provide access to the state forest from the state highway system.
Document 4: M-125 is a 19 1/2-mile-long state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan.
The highway runs through rural farmlands and connects a couple of smaller towns with Monroe.
Document 5: State Route 530 is a Washington state highway in Snohomish and Skagit counties.
The 50.52-mile long route runs northeast from an interchange with Interstate 5.
Document 6: Old Baltimore Pike is a road in the U.S. state of Delaware.
The road runs from Maryland Route 281 at the Maryland state line south of Newark, Delaware.
Document 7: State Route 716 is a state highway in the western part of the U.S. state of Ohio.
The highway was assigned in 1937 on a routing from SR 705 in Osgood to an unnumbered highway in Franklin Township.

topic.name: Film and Entertainment Industry
topic.description: This cluster encompasses notable figures and works in the film and entertainment industry, highlighting actors, directors, and significant films.

Document 1: Al Pacino is an American actor of stage and screen, filmmaker, and screenwriter.
Pacino has had a career spanning fifty years, during which time he has received numerous accolades.
Document 2: Inception is a 2010 science fiction heist thriller film written, co-produced, and directed by Christopher Nolan.
The film stars Leonardo DiCaprio as a professional thief who steals information by infiltrating the subconscious.
Document 4: Harry Shearer is an American actor, voice actor, comedian, writer, musician, author, radio host, director and producer.
He is known for his long-running roles on The Simpsons.
Document 5: Licence to Kill, released in 1989, is the sixteenth entry in the James Bond film series by Eon Productions.
It is the fifth and final consecutive Bond film to be directed by John Glen.
Document 6: Who Framed Roger Rabbit is a 1988 American live-action/animated fantasy comedy film directed by Robert Zemeckis.
The film tells the story of Eddie Valiant, a private detective who must exonerate animated character Roger Rabbit.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling Careers
topic.description: This cluster discusses the careers and achievements of various professional wrestlers in organizations like WWE and TNA.

Document 1: John Cena is an American professional wrestler, rapper, actor, and reality television show host signed to WWE.
Cena has won 24 championships, with 15 reigns as a world champion.
Document 2: Madison Rayne is an American professional wrestler currently signed to Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA).
She has also made appearances in Wrestlicious as Amber Lively.
Document 3: MNM was a villainous professional wrestling stable in WWE and OVW.
They captured the OVW Southern Tag Team Championship on one occasion.
Document 4: Wendi Richter is a retired American professional wrestler who held the WWF Women’s Championship twice.
She was involved in a storyline with singer Cyndi Lauper called the 'Rock 'n' Wrestling Connection.'
Document 5: Layla El is a retired professional wrestler best known for her time in WWE.
In May 2010, Layla won the WWE Women’s Championship for the first time.
Document 7: No Surrender (2008) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by TNA.
The main event was a Three Ways to Glory match for the TNA World Heavyweight Championship.

topic.name: Office and Workplace Comedy
topic.description: The documents discuss episodes from various television series that focus on comedic situations in office or workplace settings.

Document 1: The Deposition is the twelfth episode of the fourth season of the American comedy television series The Office
The series depicts the everyday lives of office employees in the Scranton, Pennsylvania branch of the fictional Dunder Mifflin Paper Company.
Document 5: Blind Date is the third episode of the first season of the American television comedy series 30 Rock.
The episode focuses on Jack Donaghy setting up Liz Lemon on a blind date.

topic.name: Biodiversity and Adaptation
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of biodiversity, including species adaptation and survival strategies in different environments.

Document 1: Scleractinia, also called stony corals or hard corals, are marine animals in the phylum Cnidaria that live on the seabed and build themselves a hard skeleton.
Many shallow-water corals contain symbiont unicellular organisms known as zooxanthellae within their tissues.
Document 2: The flora of Scotland is an assemblage of native plant species including over 1,600 vascular plants, more than 1,500 lichens and nearly 1,000 bryophytes.
Conservation of the natural environment is well developed and various organisations play an important role in the stewardship of the country 's flora.
Document 3: In evolutionary biology, mimicry is a similarity of one species to another that protects one or both.
Mimics occur in the same areas as their models.
Document 5: Underwater camouflage is the set of methods of achieving crypsis — avoidance of observation — that allows otherwise visible aquatic organisms to remain unnoticed by other organisms such as predators or prey.
Natural selection has produced a wide variety of methods of survival in the oceans.
Document 6: Vipera berus, the common European adder or common European viper, is a venomous snake that is extremely widespread.
The common adder is found in different terrains, habitat complexity being essential for different aspects of its behaviour.
Document 7: The Murray cod is an important part of Australia's vertebrate wildlife and is found in the Murray-Darling River system in Australia.
Murray cod populations have declined severely since European colonisation of Australia due to a number of causes, including severe overfishing, river regulation, and habitat degradation.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Influences
topic.description: This topic encompasses the exploration of significant cultural, historical, and philosophical figures and movements that have shaped societies across different eras and regions.

1: Sara Northrup Hollister was an occultist who played a major role in the creation of Dianetics, which evolved into the religious movement Scientology.
Sara was a major figure in the Pasadena branch of the Ordo Templi Orientis, a secret society founded by Aleister Crowley.
2: Elias Abu Shabaki was a Lebanese writer, poet, editor, translator, and literary critic, considered one of the leading figures of the Arabic Nahda Movement.
Abu Shabaki's poems were deeply personal and often contained biblical overtones centering on his internal moral conflicts.
3: The Han dynasty was a period of great economic, technological, cultural, and social progress in China.
Its society was governed by an emperor who shared power with an official bureaucracy and semi-feudal nobility.
4: The Demands of Liberal Education establishes a liberal political theory of children's education that fits the mutual needs of the state and its diverse citizenry.
Levinson advocates for a weak perfectionist state that can promote thick autonomy while accepting citizens who do not agree.
5: Section 3 of the Human Rights Act 1998 requires courts to interpret legislation in a way that is compatible with the articles of the European Convention of Human Rights.
Courts have applied section 3 through forms of interpretation such as 'reading in' and 'reading out.'
6: Edward Said was a Palestinian-American literary theoretician and a founder of post-colonial studies.
His book Orientalism critically analyzes the culturally inaccurate representations that form the basis of Orientalism.
7: The royal touch was a form of laying on of hands by monarchs intended to cure subjects of diseases.
This claimed power was exercised by monarchs to demonstrate the legitimacy of their reign.

topic.name: Pop and R&B Music
topic.description: The documents discuss various songs and artists within the pop and R&B genres, highlighting their musical styles, production, and chart performances.

Document 1: 'Get Me Bodied' is an R & B and bounce song with dancehall and reggae influences.
The song was generally well received by contemporary music critics.
Document 2: 'Better than Today' is a pop song that emulates other styles using guitars, synthesizers and drum programming.
It became Minogue's sixth number-one on the US Hot Dance Club Songs chart.
Document 4: 'Beautiful People' is an uptempo song which draws from the genres of electro house, dancehall and Europop.
The song received generally positive reviews from critics.
Document 5: 'Since U Been Gone' is an uptempo pop rock, and power pop song that infuses electronic sound.
Critics considered the song to be the highlight of Breakaway.
Document 6: 'Love Sex Magic' embraces a dance-pop sound, while including R & B and funk.
Critics gave the song mixed reviews, complimenting the song's funk and retro feel.

topic.name: Video Game Series Overview
topic.description: A collection of notable video games across various genres and platforms, highlighting their development, gameplay, and cultural impact.

Document 1: Mario & Luigi : Superstar Saga is a role-playing video game developed by AlphaDream and published by Nintendo for the Game Boy Advance in 2003.
The game was later re-released for the Wii U Virtual Console on the Nintendo eShop in 2014.
Document 2: Mirror 's Edge is an action-adventure platformer developed by EA DICE and published by Electronic Arts.
The game allows a greater freedom of movement with regard to its 3D environment.
Document 3: Soul Reaver 2 is an action-adventure game developed by Crystal Dynamics and published by Eidos Interactive.
Critics praised the game for its involved storyline, visuals and puzzles.
Document 4: Sonic the Hedgehog 2 is a platform video game developed by Sonic Team and published by Sega for the Sega Genesis console.
The game is the second main entry in the Sonic the Hedgehog series.
Document 5: Wipeout Pure is a futuristic racing video game developed by Sony Studio Liverpool and published by Sony Computer Entertainment for the PlayStation Portable.
The game received positive reviews from critics upon release.
Document 6: Drakengard is a series of action role-playing video games.
The series is considered highly popular in Japan, having sold well and gained a cult following.
Document 7: Mother is a role-playing video game developed by Ape and published by Nintendo for the Famicom.
The game follows the young Ninten as he uses his great-grandfather's studies on psychic powers.

topic.name: Cultural Heritage and Architecture
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical structures and their significance in cultural heritage, showcasing architectural styles and historical contexts.

Document 1: Mystic Seaport Light is a lighthouse at the south end of Mystic Seaport
designed by William F. Herman Jr. and constructed in 1966.
Document 3: Beiyue Temple is a Daoist temple located in Quyang, Hebei Province, China.
the Dening Hall of the temple is the largest, earliest and one of the most important extant wooden buildings built in the Yuan Dynasty.
Document 4: The Church Administration Building serves as the headquarters of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
completed in 1917, the building is adjacent to Temple Square.
Document 7: The Gregorian Tower was built between 1578 and 1580
to promote the study of astronomy for the Gregorian Calendar Reform.

topic.name: Atlantic Hurricanes Overview
topic.description: The documents provide a historical account and impact analysis of various Atlantic hurricanes from different years, detailing their formation, path, intensity, and effects on affected regions.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Dean was a strong tropical storm that affected at least twelve islands along its path from the tropical Atlantic Ocean to east of Atlantic Canada in August 2001.
The storm produced light to moderate rainfall in Bermuda and later in Newfoundland, though no serious damage was reported.
Document 2: The 1933 Cuba – Bahamas hurricane was last of six major hurricanes in the active 1933 Atlantic hurricane season.
Advance warning in the country prevented any storm-related fatalities, although four people suspected of looting were shot and killed during a curfew in Havana.
Document 3: Hurricane Joyce caused minor impact in the ABC and Windward islands during late September and early October 2000.
Overall, impact from Joyce was minimal, limited to mainly rainfall and near-tropical storm force winds in Trinidad and Tobago and Barbados.
Document 4: Hurricane Isidore was the ninth named storm and the second hurricane in the 2002 Atlantic hurricane season.
Its primary impact was the heavy rainfall which fell across southeast Mexico and from the central United States Gulf coast into the Ohio Valley.
Document 5: Hurricane Leslie was an Atlantic tropical cyclone that had minor effects in Bermuda and Atlantic Canada in September 2012.
In Atlantic Canada, the storm brought heavy rainfall to both Nova Scotia and Newfoundland.
Document 6: The 1933 Cuba – Brownsville hurricane was one of two storms in the 1933 Atlantic hurricane season to reach the intensity of a Category 5 strength.
Damage was estimated at $11 million.
Document 7: The 1815 North Carolina hurricane caused the most severe flooding in New Bern, North Carolina since 1795.
Overall, the hurricane inflicted at least 15 deaths throughout its existence, and more than $60,000 (1815 USD) in property damage.

topic.name: Military Engagements in History
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant battles and sieges throughout various historical periods and regions, focusing on military strategies, outcomes, and implications.

Document 1: The Battle of Bunker Hill was fought on June 17, 1775, during the Siege of Boston in the early stages of the American Revolutionary War.
Though the battle was a tactical victory for the British, the colonial forces demonstrated resilience.
Document 2: The Siege of Sparta took place in 272 BC and was a battle fought between Epirus, led by King Pyrrhus, and an alliance consisting of Sparta.
The Spartans were able to withstand the Epiriote assaults until the arrival of the main Spartan army.
Document 3: The 20th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the New Zealand Military Forces, which served during the Second World War.
It participated in the Battles of Greece and later in Crete.
Document 4: The 1704 Raid on Deerfield occurred during Queen Anne's War when French and Native American forces attacked the English frontier settlement.
The raid was a clear victory for the French coalition that aimed to take captives.
Document 5: The Siege of Fort Stanwix began on August 2, 1777, and ended August 22, during the American Revolutionary War.
The siege was finally broken when American reinforcements under the command of Benedict Arnold neared.
Document 6: The Battle of Forts Clinton and Montgomery was an American Revolutionary War battle fought on October 6, 1777.
British forces captured Fort Clinton and Fort Montgomery, creating a diversion to draw American troops.
Document 7: Walter Bedell Smith was a senior officer of the United States Army who served during World War II.
He was Eisenhower's chief of staff at Allied Forces Headquarters during the Tunisia Campaign.

topic.name: College Sports Championships
topic.description: The documents discuss various college sports championships, focusing on football and their notable events and athletes.

Document 1: 2010 ACC Championship Game was a college football game between the Virginia Tech Hokies and the Florida State Seminoles.
Virginia Tech defeated Florida State, winning the Atlantic Coast Conference football championship, 44 – 33.
Document 2: 2009 Alabama Crimson Tide football team represented the University of Alabama in the 2009 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
They finished the season undefeated with a record of 14 – 0 and as national champions.
Document 7: 2010 Michigan Wolverines football team represented the University of Michigan in the 2010 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
After its week 10 win against Illinois, Michigan became bowl eligible for the first time since 2007.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclone Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclone seasons across different ocean basins, detailing the formation, impact, and statistics of storms during those years.

Document 1: The 1957 Atlantic hurricane season was a generally inactive year for tropical cyclogenesis in the North Atlantic basin.
The strongest hurricane of the year was Carrie, which reached the equivalent of a Category 4 hurricane.
Document 2: The 2002 – 03 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was one of the longest lasting and the second-most active season in the South-West Indian ocean.
Delfina damaged or destroyed thousands of houses in Mozambique and Malawi, killing 54 people.
Document 3: The 1998 Atlantic hurricane season was one of the deadliest Atlantic hurricane seasons that featured the highest number of storm-related fatalities in over 200 years.
Mitch is also the second deadliest Atlantic hurricane in recorded history.
Document 4: The 2015 Atlantic hurricane season was a slightly below average season that produced twelve tropical cyclones, eleven named storms, four hurricanes, and two major hurricanes.
Ana caused minor flooding and wind damage in the Carolinas and left two dead in North Carolina.
Document 5: Hurricane Calvin was one of three Pacific hurricanes on record to make landfall along the Mexican coast during the month of July.
Calvin made landfall near Manzanillo at peak strength.
Document 6: The 1990 – 91 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was fairly quiet, although activity began early and the final named storm formed at a record late date.
Cyclone Bella became the strongest storm of the season, reaching 10-minute maximum sustained winds of 155 km/h (100 mph).
Document 7: The 1986 Pacific typhoon season has no official bounds; it ran year-round in 1986, but most tropical cyclones tend to form in the northwestern Pacific Ocean between May and December.
A total of 32 tropical depressions formed in 1986 in the Western Pacific over an eleven-month time span.

topic.name: Historical Figures in Governance and Religion
topic.description: This topic encompasses notable historical figures who played significant roles in governance and religious contexts during the medieval and early modern periods.

Document 1: Constantine was the third son of the Byzantine emperor Romanos I Lekapenos and co-emperor from 924 to 945.
He was overthrown and exiled by the legitimate emperor Constantine VII.
Document 2: Ranulf Flambard was a medieval Norman Bishop of Durham and an influential government minister of King William Rufus of England.
He was considered the first Chief Justiciar of England.
Document 3: Susanna Cole was the lone survivor of an Indian attack in which many of her siblings and her famed mother, Anne Hutchinson, were killed.
She was taken captive and raised by the Indians before being traded back to the English.
Document 4: Robert of Melun was an English scholastic Christian theologian who taught in France, and later became Bishop of Hereford in England.
He was involved in the dispute between Becket and the king.
Document 5: Richard Bellingham was a colonial magistrate, lawyer, and several-time governor of the Massachusetts Bay Colony.
He was one of the architects of the Massachusetts Body of Liberties.
Document 6: Miguel de Azcuénaga was an Argentine brigadier and member of the Primera Junta, the first autonomous government of modern Argentina.
He held several offices since then, most notably being the first Governor intendant of Buenos Aires.
Document 7: Æthelwold was a 7th-century king of East Anglia, a member of the Wuffingas dynasty.
He lived during a time of political and religious upheaval in East Anglia.

topic.name: Rock Music Albums and Themes
topic.description: The documents discuss various influential rock music albums and their thematic content, including personal and social issues reflected in the lyrics and music.

the ending guitar solo was a cathartic experience for the Edge.

the band watched news reports from Iran, including scenes of repression and curfews.

its songs have remained classic rock staples.

features the band performing extended jam versions of songs.

helped gain international support for Bangladeshi independence.

exploring themes of abandonment and personal isolation.

topic.name: Science Fiction Television Series
topic.description: The documents explore various science fiction television series and their characters, focusing on their narratives, themes, and production details.

1: 'Meat' is the fourth episode of the second series of British science fiction television series Torchwood
Torchwood initially depicts a small team of alien-hunters known as Torchwood Three
3: 'Pilot' is the pilot episode of the science fiction television series The X-Files
The episode follows FBI Special Agents Mulder and Scully on their first X-File case together, investigating a string of deaths which Mulder believes to be alien experiments.
6: Dr. Helen Magnus is the series protagonist and central character of the Canadian fantasy-science-fiction television series Sanctuary
Magnus runs the global Sanctuary Network, an organization tasked with finding a series of creatures called 'abnormals'.
